text: Serial processing in the brain is not simply a limitation — it is a feature that enables depth. Because the prefrontal cortex allocates its executive bandwidth to a single task stream, that stream receives the full force of the brain's most sophisticated machinery: working memory, inhibitory control, goal maintenance, and error monitoring. Shallow parallel engagement, by contrast, spreads these resources thin, reducing every thread to a surface pass. Cal Newport's "deep work" framework is, at its neurological core, a claim about serial processing: to think genuinely hard about something, you must allow the brain's serial executive system to settle fully onto one problem and stay there.
question: According to the passage, what neurological advantage does serial processing provide over shallow parallel engagement?

options.text: It activates the hippocampus more reliably, improving long-term memory encoding
It allows the full force of the brain's executive machinery to be directed at one task
It reduces the metabolic cost of cognition by limiting glucose consumption
It enables faster task completion by eliminating the need for error monitoring
